name: Cooper, Harry
motto: "It's not hard for me to kick against the pricks."
description: A pencil, a note book and a laugh constitute the barricade behind which we always see Mr. Cooper. He is fond of discussions, both learned and otherwise, and perhaps for this reason he has been dubbed "our knocker." However, he is a broad minded student, always alert and working for the best interests of the University.

name: Aakhus, Daniel
motto: "I am not without suspicion that I have an undeveloped faculty of music within me."
description: Mr. Aakhus came to the University from Bygland, Minnesota, and took his preparatory work here. He is quiet and unassuming and an excellent student.

name: Brannon, Marshall
motto: "Maidens count me for a stranger."
description: We might call him our athlete, our bachelor, our wit, or our book store magnate, for he has become famous in all four roles. He has been a regular player on the football team since '02, on the baseball team since '04, and has made a creditable showing in basketball. He has been a member of the Athletic Board of Control for five years. In his Sophomore year "Marsh" brought honor to himself and his class by his gallant defense of the '07 flag on the power house chimney and his share in detaining the '06 toastmaster.

name: Carkin, John
motto: "The sweetest hours that e'er I spend, are spent among the lasses."
description: John came to us from the U. of M. in 1904. He early distinguished himself by his ability to manage anything from a basketball game to the most complicated love affair. He has lost his heart beyond the shadow of a doubt, the only question is—to whom.

name: Davis, Forrest M.
motto: "There must be some good hard work in him—none ever came out."
description: Forrest is a native of Sykeston, N. D. He obtained his high school education at the University, where he at once became popular. He is noted for his pluck and courage on the football field, and his fondness for a certain fair "schoolmarm" at Doyon.

name: Duggan, Susie
motto: "Maiden with the meek blue eyes."
description: Susie started the year as a Junior, but after consulting Dr. Thomas decided to finish it with the Seniors. Her sweet temper and amiable disposition have endeared her to us all.

name: Inberg, John Emil
motto: "A steady, sober sort of a citizen."
description: Mr. Inberg took his high school work in the Classical Gymnasium of his native village, Kuopio, Finland. He has always been a hard worker and a faithful student. His favorite diversion is the study of mankind.

name: Grandy, Louis M.
motto: "I know a trick worth two of that."
description: "Our sport" was born in Springville, Ontario, but has developed into a thorough Dakotan and University man. He is said to be death on ducks and prairie chickens, and is noted for his sleight of hand performances.

name: Robertson, Lois
motto: "But still her tongue ran on."
description: When Wesley College became affiliated with the University Lois joined us. Her pleasantness and sociability make her a general favorite.
text:
"But still her tongue ran on."

name: Kirk, Ida B.
motto: "As full of jokes as an egg is of meat."
description: Ida is our class historian; she is also our class wit and "knocks" in this history can be safely laid at her door. She is Local Editor of the Student, and morning, noon and night her slogan is, "Say, don't you know any new joshes for the Student?"

name: McFarland, Elsie W.
motto: "Would there were more like her."
description: When a Junior, in 1904, Elsie became famous as King Dodo's heroine. She left the "U" for a year, but came back for her senior work with the determination to become a model housekeeper. She is already mistress of the art of fudge making and will doubtless achieve her ambition before the year is out.

name: O'Connor, J. F. T.
motto: "A fine volley of words, gentlemen, and easily shot off."
clubs: Winner Local and State Oratorical Contests 1907
description: Frank did not wake up one morning and find himself famous, for fame came to him only after persistent and enthusiastic work in the interest of his Alma Mater. He is our orator, our journalist, our organizer, and also our Irishman, for we are all sure that he has at least seen the "Blarney stone."

motto: "Spoke like an oracle, they all exclaim, and add Right Reverend to his name."
description: Mr. Pippy came to the University in '05, having obtained his preliminary education at the R. R. V. U. While there he is said to have distinguished himself by his fighting ability.

name: Vorachek, Pauline A.
motto: "She is a perfect knowledge box; an oracle to great and small."
description: This young lady was a freshman in the fall of 1905, but early in 1906 joined the Junior class. She says she came to the "U" to discover the minimum of work with which one could graduate; she certainly has discovered the minimum of time. Classes under Mr. Koch are her favorite diversion.

name: Ruud, Magnus B.
motto: "And his hair was something sandy, And was done in knotty curls, And was parted in the middle, In the manner of a girl's."
description: Magnus, "the Great," is following in the footsteps of his brother and graduating in three years. Of all the events of the school year he enjoys the football trip to Fargo the most, doubtless because of the welcome he receives when he gets there.

name: Shanley, Helen S.
motto: "Could jest in ancient Greek."
description: Helen is one of our bright girls, for she was never known to get less than an A. She is noted for being half of Professor Pease's famous Greek team.

name: Wickham, Ray E.
high school: Glen Ullin, N. D., and Valparaiso, Indiana
motto: "An amiable man, as all could testify."
description: He has the distinction of being our only Mining Engineer, and also our only inventor.
text:
"An amiable man, as all could testify."
He has the distinction of being our only Mining Engineer, and also our only inventor.

name: Thacker, Robert
motto: "He is the very pineapple of politeness."
description: R. R. V. U. gave him his first two years of college work, but hearing of the "naughty seven" class he at once came here to join it. He is very fond of the society of the young ladies—so much so indeed that he wants a whole "Roe" for himself.

name: Macilraith, Evan J.
motto: "My heart's in the Highlands."
hometown: a pretty village in the "Highlands of Ontario"
description: "Scotie," strange to say, is not a Scot at all, but a "Canuck," for he was born in a pretty village in the "Highlands of Ontario." Lanark, Ontario, gave him his high school education and sent us a diligent and conscientious student, who, nevertheless, has plenty of time to give to all society and class affairs.

name: McGauvran, Gordon
motto: "Past hope, past cure, past help."
description: "Terry" is one of the loyal members of the class who, believing that one can't get too much of a good thing, took his preparatory work at the University. He has been an enthusiastic member of the baseball team since 1903, and has had a share in basketball also. His vocation and avocation is strolling on the track with his Ward.

name: Ward, Cecil C.
motto: "Lock the doors upon a woman's wit And it will out at the casement."
description: Whatever she does is done well, may truthfully be said of Cecil. For three years she was a member of the girls' basketball team, and for two years was captain. She has been on the winning teams of two Gansl medal debates, has taken an active interest in Y. W. C. A. work and in all University activities. She is a thorough student and, altogether, a "jolly good fellow," ready for any enterprise. Her one weakness is a fondness for red hair.

name: Clouston, Daisy
motto: "Rich in saving common sense."
description: Behind a quiet exterior this young lady hides a keen sense of humor and an independent spirit. Early in life she conceived the idea of obtaining a doctor's degree and has worked steadily on with that aim in view.

name: Henry Wergeland Blichfeldt
motto: "A poet's name I bear."
description: Born and raised in the Red River Valley. He has attended the schools in Grand Forks since childhood, with the exception of one year which he spent in California. He was a member of last year's class, but decided to wait for this class and make a specialty of Real Property.

name: David P. Bates
motto: "The man who holds his own—at the Homestead."
description: He is a loyal member of the Forum and is its present President. He is a graduate of the Larimore High School and was a former student at the University. He played on the '03 football team and is one of the most prominent of our members in social circles.

name: G. A. Brauer
motto: "A comfortable man with dividends."
description: Our genial Class President has been engaged in the land business at Fessenden, where he has also held public office. He secured his education at Luther College, Decorah, Iowa. He is a good student, popular, discreet and unassuming, and a favorite not only among his fellow students, but among ladies of all nationalities.

name: E. W. Burke
motto: "Noblest Roman of them all."
description: Mr. Burke is a graduate of the Crystal High School. He is the Clerk of the Moot Court and one of the Librarians. He was a sailor for four years on the Great Lakes, and got on to the mysteries of the labor union and the horror of the strikes. He intends to practice law for the sake of promoting justice and not for the money there is in it.

name: Olger B. Burtness
motto: "So much a man can do who does both act and know."
description: There are few student activities in which "Omnibus Olger" has not at some time participated. He has taken part in several inter-society and two inter-collegiate debates. He has won laurels on the gridiron and was captain of the '06 football team. Last year he got his degree and a prize for scholarship. This year he is postmaster and sells books for revenue. His other works are too numerous to mention.

name: Asa E. Crothers
motto: "Let's take a bald case."
description: Mr. Crothers is a native of Wisconsin. After graduating from the Mayville Normal he was high school principal for some time. He then entered the civil service as a railway postal clerk and has since followed that employment. He entered the Law School during the winter term last year.

name: John L. Cashel, Jr.
high school: High School in Grafton, Culver Military Academy, Culver, Ind., St. Thomas College, St. Paul
motto: "Variety is the spice of life."
description: This staunch democrat comes from Grafton. He has attended the High School in his own town, the Culver Military Academy, Culver, Ind., and St. Thomas College, St. Paul. A year ago he registered at the "U," but he liked the Law School so well that his course was changed from Arts to Law.

name: John P. Conmy
motto: "Push on—keep moving."
description: "Chick" comes from Pembina and is a graduate of the High School of his home town and of the University. He is an all-round athlete, star football player and captain of the '06 and '07 baseball teams. He excels in basketball and track work—strolling on the railroad track included.

name: George W. Graff
high school: Highland Park College, Des Moines, Iowa
motto: "To what happy accident is it that we owe so unexpected a visit?"
description: Mr. Graff comes from Cooperstown. He has studied at Highland Park College, Des Moines, Iowa, and was afterwards admitted to the Oregon Bar. He became a member of this class at the beginning of the present school year.

name: Nels O. Dolve
motto: "The man who loves and laughs must sure do well."
description: Mr. Dolve, from Portland-on-the-Goose, is a graduate of the Mayville Normal and the "U." He is president of the oratorical association and member of the debating board of control. At the 17th of May celebration, last spring, he delivered a speech in the Norwegian language. It is predicted that his first case will be a merger case.

name: Ingvald, Knudson
motto: "So far shall you go and no further!"—King Knute.
clubs: Musician and assistant director of the University Band
description: He has attended the public schools of Becker County. Any one visiting moot court would say that Knute was cut out for a sheriff. He is a terror to the juniors and unhesitatingly lays down the Law (?) to the unwary.

name: Gunderson, O. S.
motto: "A very unclubable man."
description: Mr. Gunderson secured his education at the Moorhead Normal and the Science School at Wahpeton. He is the Fitzsimmons of the class. This was demonstrated when in a class row he got into entangling relations with the referee. He is assisting a local attorney in answering phone calls and writing complaints.

name: Hawver, Otho R.
motto: "The only way to have a friend is to be one."
clubs: Member of a partnership known as Damon and Pythias
description: On his way to North Dakota from Wisconsin, his domicile of origin, Mr. Hawver has sojourned the greater part of his life at Barnesville, Minn. At this place he has been engaged in the lumber and banking business.

name: Husband, W. C.
motto: "To try thy Eloquence, now it is time!"
clubs: Member of the debating board of control
description: He whom every one calls "Billy," our illustrious Orator, hails from Hensel, a political stronghold. Billy has been a student at the University for the past four years and has won laurels both in oratory and debate. He took first place in the local oratorical contest in '06, and was on the team which debated Montana last winter.

motto: "'Tis not my profit that does lead mine honor; mine honor, it."
clubs: Member of the debating board of control
description: He is a graduate of the Valley City Normal School and the "U;" he has taken part in two inter-collegiate debates. At the Law School he is known as the "Book Store Grafter," but at the "U" his ingratiating smile has made him known as the handsome man and the ladies' man.

name: Marquette, John J. P.
motto: "And still be doing, never done."
description: He has attended the Union University at Albany, where he incidentally played football. Last spring he won a valuable prize for scholarship. It is intimated that Jack has found his Jill.

name: McCurdy, Fred E.
motto: "But it is still best that a mile should contain 1,760 yards."
description: Fred is a graduate of the University, '06; he is of a practical turn of mind. Last year he was business manager of the "Weekly Student;" this year his attention is more centered upon the "Daily Student."

motto: "A minister but still a man."
clubs: Member of the debating board of control
description: Our pulpit orator comes originally from Ontario and settled in North Dakota in the early days. He has attended Hamline and Northwestern Universities. He has a standing offer to any of his classmates to the effect that he will perform the ceremony free of charge. The new students go to him for second-hand books.

name: McKinnon, Stevenson D.
motto: "So many cares has he as to give him an air of responsibility and dignity."
description: Our Scotch Class Vice-President comes from Mapleton. Preparatory to the study of law he attended the A. C. and University. The success of the law school dances is in a large measure due to his management. To him are given the keys of—the law school library.

name: Lyman, L. Miller
motto: "A small spark sometimes makes a big fire."
description: The little corporal comes from Fargo, where he has attended Fargo College several years. He is a basketball player and has done several other athletic "stunts" at the Y. M. C. A. building. He cracks jokes, quotes Scripture, Caesar and Cicero, and yet enlarges his vocabulary by valuable additions of Scandinavian words, phrases and interjections. He is a ladies' man and a salesman.

name: Henry, Moen
motto: "A man he seems of cheerful yesterdays and confident tomorrows."
description: Before entering the law school this year Mr. Moen has studied at the University of Minnesota, both in the Academic and Law Department. He is illustrative of the proposition that a cool head and steady hand will bring a man to the front. His only bad habit is a daily stroll up North Third Street.

name: S. O., Oistad
motto: "The world is a wheel and it will all come round right."
description: Mr. Oistad came to the "U" two years ago as a graduate from the Fosston, Minnesota, High School. Last year he attended the University of Minnesota Law School, and entered this class at the beginning of the present school year to complete his law course. He represents the Philomathian Society on the debating board of control.

name: Fred, A. Oliver
motto: "The future smiling and the past unknown."
description: "Twisty" has not unbosomed himself sufficiently to throw much light as to his past. We only know that he comes from Lisbon and has attended the University of Minnesota Law School two years. He comes to us this year, and since then we have kept track of him and know that he played on the '06 football team and is now practicing basketball.

He secured his education at Pillsbury Academy and Breck College, and after graduation he settled in McLean County, North Dakota, and founded the city of McClusky on his homestead. He is a musician, the Paderewski of the class, and a successful business man with an income at his heels.

name: A. M., Thompson
high school: Iowa State Normal and Lincoln University
motto: "Still he keeps o' the windy side of the law."
description: He was born in Illinois, but has spent the greater part of his life at Nevada, Ia., securing his education at the Iowa State Normal and Lincoln University. He has wielded the pointer and rod for four years. His ambition is to enter the business world and own a herd of pedigree stock.

name: T. A., Thompson
motto: "A man who undertakes considerable things, even in a regular way, ought to give us grounds to presume ability."
description: Mr. Thompson entered the class this year and comes from Cooperstown. He has been a student of the University of Michigan for two years. He is a genial and all-round good fellow and usually establishes his point in an argument.

name: J. K., Murray
motto: "The light that lies in woman's eyes, has been my heart's undoing."
clubs: Chief dance promoter in mass meeting
description: Mr. Murray is a native of Wisconsin, and has been teaching and homesteading in the western part of this state. He has attended the University of Wisconsin and the summer school at the U. N. D. He was a member of the Forum debating team this year. Lately he has been elected chief dance promoter in mass meeting, and the result was a dance the next evening. He is noted for premature explosions.

name: E. C., Thomas
high school: Madison High School, Valley City Normal
motto: Query— "Would this come under the Statute of Frauds?"
description: Mr. Thomas was born in Wisconsin, is a graduate of the Madison High School and the Valley City Normal. He came to South Dakota at an early age and later moved to Valley City. Before entering the law school he was a wielder of the birch.

name: N. J., Noble
motto: "I am for men."
description: Mr. Noble, of Knox, is another of our successful business men. He was a student of the University some years ago. When the Spanish American War broke out he enlisted and was sent to Cuba. Some of the ladies, however, seemed to think that it was the Civil War he served in. He is always ready to uphold the protective tariff on principle.

name: Shubeck, Franz Elough
motto: "What stronger breastplate than a soul untainted? Thrice armed is he whose cause is just."
description: Now he hails from Kidder County of this state. He would have studied law in South Dakota, but the spirit did not move him till after he had crossed the line. He owns land in three parishes. He is a knight of the simple life and a student of Shakespeare. He admires women who are taxpayers.

name: Vetleson, Martin
high school: Park Region Luther College, Fergus Falls, Minn
motto: "He simply clears his throat and then—runs lightly up the scale."
description: Like so many of the members of the class he has taught school for several years. He is an industrious young man and believes that every man should wed his profession.

name: Ware, Thomas A.
motto: "The man with the goods."
description: He spends his time in the companionship of his books in the study of law and economic questions. He has established a reputation as a hard worker and as a debater. Last year he was on the Forum debating team and on the Manitoba debating team. He is also taking postgraduate work at the "U."

name: Warmer, B. F.
high school: high school in '99 with the class of the Klondyke Mind
motto: "There is something rotten in the state of Denmark."—Hamlet
description: His earliest recollection is that he came to North Dakota six months old. He graduated from the high school in '99 with the class of the Klondyke Mind. He has taught school for some time. His ardent mode of expression was a valuable asset to him in making stump speeches during the recent campaign.

name: Keating, Peter L.
motto: "He bears him like a portly gentleman."
description: His present domicile is Wahpeton, N. D. He is the latest acquisition to our class.
text:
"He bears him like a portly gentleman."

name: Zavitovsky, Louis A.
motto: "For short he is called Butt-insky."
description: Afterwards he taught school for three years, and for a short time attended the St. Paul College of Law. He entered the Law School in the fall of '06.
text:

name: Finley, Orma
motto: "As merry as the day is long."
description: She is a diligent worker and her cheerful disposition is a never failing characteristic.
text:
"As merry as the day is long."
She is a diligent worker and her cheerful disposition is a never failing characteristic.

name: Barrington, Hazel
motto: "Oh, such dark eyes! a single glance of them Will govern a whole life from birth till death!"
description: She has shown herself a brilliant student and has taken a prominent part in University activities. In the Gansl Medal Debate for '06 she won honors for herself and her society. But we like her especially for her charming manner and personality, which has proved too much even for the ex-president of the Athletic Association.

name: Bogan, Katie
motto: "She smiled on many, just for fun; I knew there was nothing in it."
description: She is noted for having pretty hair and eyes, and spending Saturday nights in the parlor where she doubtless enjoys many "Blissful" moments.
text:
"She smiled on many, just for fun; I knew there was nothing in it."

name: Fiering, Ida
motto: "What shall I do to be forever known?"
description: This damsel has imbibed knowledge at both Cooperstown and Fargo High Schools, which accounts partly for her being more than an ordinary student. She was a member of the '06 Normal class, but stayed out one year to be able to graduate with the class of '07. Her greatest fault is the wearing of an A. C. pin and a diamond.

name: Finley, Elsa
motto: "Gone daft over a man! and she an educated woman."
description: She has announced that her ambition is to get married, and if appearances are not too deceiving she will probably realize her ambition. Noted for being Prof. Koch's pianist.

name: Hagen, Isabel
motto: "To teach or to marry; that is the question."
description: She is reported to be an accomplished cook and housekeeper, an excellent thing in woman. Moreover, she is noted for her fluent use of polysyllabic words.

"To teach or to marry; that is the question."

name: Hayes, Helen
motto: "Her face is fair, her heart is true, As spotless as she's bonnie."
description: She joined this class in '05, and has ever since proved a loyal class mate. Her ambition is to attend a West Point Military Academy Hop.
text:
"Her face is fair, her heart is true, As spotless as she's bonnie."

name: Harstad, Laura
motto: "I write much, but what of it."
description: After taking a good look at the Normal class, however, she decided to cast her lot with it. She is a thorough student. Her avocation is letter writing.

name: Heath, N. Pearl
motto: "Give her but the least excuse to love."
description: She is said to be an inspiration to certain literary men, such as the editor of the Delaware College Annual.
text:
"Give her but the least excuse to love."

name: Lovett, Laura
motto: "Nor bold, nor shy, nor short, nor tall, but a new mingling of them all."
description: Laura is one of the University's most faithful friends, having spent almost four whole years on its campus. She, having begun her work here, decided to be faithful to the end. Her work as a student is highly praised. She is particularly noted for her melodeon.

name: Jesten, Ingline
motto: "A soul in earnest."
description: This element in our class came from Castleton High School. She has the reputation of being a faithful student. She is a charter member of the Clionian Literary Society.
text:
"A soul in earnest."

name: Murchie, Mayme
motto: "Not to idle dreams and trances, Length of face and solemn tone, But to faith in daily striving And performance shown."
description: Langdon High School must have done its work well when it laid the foundation for her education, for a more earnest and faithful student cannot be found. She was a delegate to the Y. W. C. A. state convention at Valley City, '06.

name: Mclean, Agnes
motto: "A skillful mistress in her art."
clubs: Cabinet Member Y. W. C. A.
description: This little Scotch girl came from Hannah in the early ages, and has succeeded in completing her preparatory work and becoming a member of the senior class. She represented the Y. W. C. A. at the summer conference at Waterloo in '06, and at the state convention in '05. Her artistic skill has made her one of the prominent Dacotah illustrators. She advises those who are not so wise in the ways of the world to cultivate the social side of life, especially in the balmy days of spring, a thing which has ever brought comfort to her.

name: Olson, Aletta
motto: "The more the merrier."
description: Lettie is one of the social lights of Grafton, N. D. Graduated in '05, and came to the "U" the following year; represented the Y. W. C. A. at the state convention in '06. She seems to have struck upon a happy medium between working and bluffing, for neither seems to interfere with her enjoyment of life. We like Lettie best because she is a jolly, good companion.

motto: "Work is my recreation."
description: Miss Ratcliffe is a Canadian, but her home is now at Medford, N. D. She came to the University for her preparatory work, and all her career at the "U" has shown her to be a deep student. She is a charter member of the Sorosis Literary Society.

name: Sand, Guri
motto: "As headstrong as an 'allegory' On the banks of the Nile."
description: Miss Sand once lived in the sunny valleys of Norway, but now claims North Dakota as her home. Her Norse blood is doubtless the reason for her unflinching energy and her affinity for hard work.

name: Wilde, Doretta
motto: "An open hearted maiden, true and pure."
description: This young lady prides herself on being one of the "Four Hundred," of Thompson, N. D. She took her preparatory education at the University, joined the freshmen class in '05, but the following year decided to graduate with the '07 Normal class. Her work is always done as it should be, for she is a diligent worker.

name: Templeton, Bertha
motto: "A woman's crown of glory is her hair."
description: Grand Forks High School claims the honor of numbering her among its alumni. She entered the freshman class in '05, but after a year's experience joined the Normal class. She is such an excellent student that one of her professors has made her his assistant.

name: Hermann, Dora
motto: "An unwieldy bundle of good nature."
description: This maid came from the snowy plains of Iceland, and has ever since dwelt in Edinburgh, N. D. Soon after entering the "U" she became active in basketball, and has played guard on the Varsity team for the past two years. Dora was sent to represent the Y. W. C. A. at the "Student Volunteer Convention," held at Nashville, Tenn. The departure of this senior will be a great loss to the "U," chiefly because of her having held the position of official chaperon.

name: Gehrkie, Minnie
motto: "True as the dial to the sun."
description: Miss Gehrkie left Mayville Normal to join this class in '06. She took her high school course in Grand Forks. She is modest and retiring, but a very hard worker.

name: Coger, Elizabeth M.
motto: "There was a soft and pensive grace; A cast of thought upon her face."
description: In 1902 this lady of Grecian face and Grecian intellect joined our little caravan on its way to the fount of wisdom. She has always been an enthusiastic admirer of athletics (as well as athletes), and has distinguished herself as a student of literature and science.

name: Bell, Anthony Joseph
motto: "He proved best man in the field, and for his mind was brow-bound with the oak."
description: "Toney" came to the institution from the University of Michigan, a sophomore, with the firm intention of becoming a mining engineer. We understand, however, that he has finally decided to turn "Baker." A springy gait, a vigorous and manly appearance, mark him a top-notcher in athletics and a lion in society.

name: Bliss, Jay Wesley
motto: "A tall man—sun-crowned."
description: Jay—a loyal man of undaunted spirit—knows the value of modesty and can effectively summon a blush at the psychological moment. He's a faithful worker and is preparing for a mining career by conscientious digging.

name: Boise, Charles Watson
motto: "He is the best ever."
description: The Editor in Chief's little laugh suggests his big, broad mind and a heart in perfect sympathy with all the really good things in life. He works and plays with untiring energy. He's one of the rare kind who finds a genuine pleasure in shaking hands with all his friends.

name: Brennan, Daniel Vincent
motto: "Great Scott! On what does this our Caesar feed that he has grown so great."
description: He rests only in bed. A winner of scholarship honors, yet a good mixer, he lends his support to all University activities and he truly enjoys college life. Daniel's eloquence—his charming flow of logic—would soothe the savage breasts in the lion's den. It's a pleasure to have Dan run the institution.

name: Evans, Amy Marsh
motto: "It's only a freckle, never mind it."
description: She's a doctor's daughter. Amy administers good medicine to every ailing form of activity with which she comes in contact. Her faithful work as an editor is representative of her entire University life.
text:
"It's only a freckle, never mind it."

name: Foley, William Henry
motto: "Speaks three or four languages, word for word, without a book."
description: Foley is one of the roving members of our class, a graduate of an Illinois High School, and he subsequently attended Highland Park College, Des Moines, Iowa. He entered the University in 1905; is an excellent Greek student, a mathematical prodigy, and is thought by many to be a professor in disguise. He's a good fellow at that.

name: Farnham, Albert Thomas
motto: "Modern wisdom plucks me from over-credulous haste."
description: Our basketball man—tall, black-haired, reserved, fond of saying nothing, but doing everybody. A good athlete, a good student. "Al" is a friend as a friend should be. He takes part in almost every college activity except "trackwork."

name: Ford, Don Sneath
motto: "Smooth runs the water where the brook runs deep."
description: Ford came to us in the fall of 1905, having done his freshman work at the Red River Valley University. He is an excellent student and a good orator. In 1906 he won the Main Declamation Contest. Don is a strong Y. M. C. A. man and a conscientious worker.

name: Faust, Charles Julius
motto: "Impartiality their talents scan, Just education forms the man."
description: "Charlie," contributed by a Dutch settlement in Pennsylvania, is a persevering and conscientious student and an enthusiast in athletics. He has chosen teaching as his profession, and his one desire in the world is a "chance to make good."

name: Lyons, Nona Emma
motto: "Hang sorrow! care will kill a cat, And therefore let's be merry."
description: This young lady took up work in the Arts course in 1904. Nona's ever cheerful and smiling face shows her kind and carefree disposition. Her clear ringing voice has made her very successful, both in debate and dramatic work.

name: Forrest, Ida Mae
motto: "Here still is the smile that no cloud can o'ercast."
description: Ida believes in sunshine philosophy. She is not dismayed at being the smallest member of the class, but wears her high heels with a graceful resignation. Her smiling presence on the Dacotah Board has helped to carry us over the rough places and has made our work much easier, but the tapping of her little hammer can be heard from afar.

name: Fuller, Stephen Jay
motto: "Of learning vast, of knowledge most profound, and best of all, a kind and courteous gentleman."
description: Jay is a native of the smiling plains of Wisconsin. He did his Freshman work in the College of Arts in the year 1899-1900. He returned to the University in 1905 and entered the Engineering College, and has distinguished himself as a practical and persevering student. He is the Edison of the class—faithful in everything.

name: Griffith, Paul Bartlett
motto: "The man who always makes good."
description: Paul is now a finished product of the Red River Valley, having been caught while still in his early youth. He is an energetic business man and his untiring efforts have contributed greatly to the success of the Dacotah.

description: Mabel has the true poetic instinct—she can run off a few verses in no time. Although decidedly partial to one of the book store magnates, still she believes that the sun rises and sets in Larimore. She is diligent in class work and a loyal Adelphian.

name: Kyllo, Henry Louis
motto: "He seemed to be going through life much pleased with everything."
description: Henry completed his preparatory work at the University, coming here first in 1899. He is a finished baseball artist and an enthusiast in other athletics. He is never "worried, or hurried, or flurried," and is the best natured man in the class.

name: Mahon, Cecil Clifford
motto: "Why so quiet in this gay world?"
description: Cecil is an old timer here, first entering the University in 1899. Though he is a member of the College of Arts he has chosen Mining subjects as his electives, and by hard "digging" he has boosted himself well along on the road to success.
text:

name: Learn, James Mowatt
motto: "Look you—I am most concerned in my own interests."
description: Jimmie is a Canadian by birth and a booster by habit. He is a devotee to the languages, both ancient and modern. He inflects the Greek verb with rare fluency; he speaks French with the true Parisian accent; and he pronounces German like a native of Hanover. He's the busiest man in the institution. No proof required; he admits it.

name: McKay, Guy R.
motto: "The fire has entered my soul and has burned me to the quick."
description: Guy is just like all the other Engineers, as busy as the day is long—except in the spring term when he seems to have as much time as anyone else to "count the ties." He is noted for his peculiar faculty of escaping the Dacotah Board Meetings, but he gets in some tall "licks" just the same. Looks as if he could hit hard if he took the notion.

name: Wendell, Homer Linwell
motto: "A gentleman and a scholar."
description: After waiting sixteen years Wendell joined the class of '08 in the fall of 1904. He achieved fame as the assistant manager of the football team in 1906, and has since joined the Ancient and Exclusive Order of "Forresters."
text:

name: Valdimar, J. Melsted
motto: "Our self-made men are the glory of our institutions."
description: After successfully climbing the north pole in company with King Knute, of the Law School, he entered the College of Mining Engineering in 1904. He has strong analytical powers, as is shown by his excellence in mathematics, and he will make a success in his chosen profession.

"Our self-made men are the glory of our institutions."

name: Charles, Earl Rodgers
motto: "I am not one of those who don't believe in love at first sight, but I believe in taking a second look."
description: Poor Earl has more troubles than any ten men here. He is the weather man of the institution and must frequently deliver bunches of fine weather, even when the other weather men have scheduled rains and blizzards. His only leisure moments are on Saturday evenings, when he may invariably be found in the parlor of Davis Hall.

name: Albert, Edward Sanderson
motto: "In mathematics he was greater Than Tycho Brahe or Erra Pater."
description: Ted is an excellent scholar and is another of our mathematical "sharks." With his knowledge of higher plane curves, parabolas and the fair sex, we are confident of his success as an engineer.

name: Albert, Edgar Selby
motto: "Describe him if you can."
description: Bert started the class in its straight and narrow path, and has done much ever since to keep it there. He plays a prominent part in musical circles and society in general, besides taking a deep interest in the Senior Normal Class.
text:
"Describe him if you can."

name: Walter, Jay Smith
motto: "I care for nobody; no, not I, if nobody cares for me."
description: Jay is our optimist, and his ever ready smile has brought hope to the hearts of his classmates ever since they organized in 1904. His motto is: "Never do anything today that you can put off until tomorrow." He is never discouraged and never angry.

name: Lynn, Redmon Sarles
motto: "The glass of fashion and the mould of form, the observed of all observers!"
description: Lynn is our Beau Brummel, graduate of Highland Park Military Academy. He can do anything from bringing forth exquisite strains of music from his cornet to keeping a "corner" on a corner in the library. He's a mighty good fellow at every stage of the game.

name: Glenn, Osborne Taylor
motto: "Laugh and the world laughs with you."
description: Glenn won fame as an athlete when captain of the basketball team, and has nobly upheld the honor of his society in debate. He is a "brother" to all the girls, having first won their hearts through his pencil sharpening capabilities.

name: Steener, Steenerson
motto: "O, let him pass! he hates him much, That would upon the rack of this tough world Stretch him out longer."
description: A man, unstunted by the weight of his name. He evidently believes that "variety is the very spice of life," for he began with Minnesota, spent part of his freshman year at the University of Michigan, part of his senior year in the University of Wisconsin, then naturally came to the University of North Dakota.

name: Woods, John Dakota
motto: "I'm running this Universe; keep off the grass."
description: He is the handsomest man in the class. He is a born benefactor, is interested in everything that goes on in the institution and likes to "boss the job." The burden of his song is, "What is home without a Gardiner."

name: Treichler, Herbert Ellsworth
motto: "'Tis the breathing time of day with me."
description: Herbie, when still a young man of 14 summers and as many hard winters, first appeared at the "U" in 1902, and by his quiet manner and good humor has made many and lasting friends. He is one of the youngest members of our class, though from his height you wouldn't know it. He is always "in" on every "spread" and contributes his share of the fun.

name: Turner, James Harlan
motto: "Gaily the Troubadour Touches his guitar."
description: James, better known as Jimmy, is like every other engineer, the busiest man in school. But that mischievous smile and the twinkle in those dark eyes has helped him through many a hard problem. His musical skill is of a high order, and also his work as a member of the track team is very commendable.

name: Wiltse, George Eugene
motto: "I dare do all that may become a man."
description: George has the distinction of being the only preacher in our class, but he already shows signs of getting over it. He comes from the Red River Valley University, is a hard, faithful worker and a good student. If you want a true friend, George is your man.

name: Warmer, George Atcheson
motto: "Seest thou a man diligent in his business, he shall stand before kings."
description: Warmer is another Junior who possesses a roving disposition. After graduating from Drayton High School he spent his freshman year at Northwestern University and entered our good old "U" in 1905. Now he is firmly convinced that North Dakota is the place for him and that there is no class like the Juniors of '08. "Atch" is a dandy good friend, but alas the girl!—he has reserved a "cosy corner in his heart for—"

name: Wyant, Maude
motto: "And in her tongue is the law of kindness."
description: "And her name was Maude." As a basketball star she needs no comment, along literary lines she is a scholar of high rank, and in social circles she is a general favorite. Maude takes an active interest in almost every activity of University life and is a true "college girl."

name: Sveinbjorn, Johnson
motto: "Think not, my friend, that the dullness of this man's eye portends the condition of his mind."
description: A man of great mind.
text:
"Think not, my friend, that the dullness of this man's eye portends the condition of his mind."
A man of great mind.

name: Bennett, Mrs. Geo.
motto: "There is a woman at the beginning of all great things."
description: From a long line of illustrious ancestors, notably the Sewards and Hills, she comes to our class to add that refinement and polish that women alone can bring. She came to North Dakota to grow up with the country. Greatness and largeness of action are her ideals.

name: Bradley, Harold C.
motto: "I have often regretted my speech, never my silence."
description: He is a son of North Dakota, having passed the sum of twenty-five years in this state. Forman is his present home. Before coming to the Law School he attended Hamline College.
text:
"I have often regretted my speech, never my silence."

name: Burke, Thomas H.
motto: "I am the only scholar from Valley City, all the rest are dubs."
clubs: President of the Junior Law Class
description: Before coming to the 'U,' he graduated from the Normal at his home town—Valley City. While at the Normal he was so influenced that he became a grafter of the first rank. Last year he was principal of the Bowbells Public Schools, but on deciding that the teaching profession was too honest, he betook himself to the Law School to become a lawyer.

name: Cameron, Scott
motto: "A tongue that never fails to speak the truth."
description: Came to the 'U' in 1903; always prominent in University life, both socially and politically. His is a career that will make 'Boss Tweed' look like a pupil of the old school. An embryo statesman, but still honest.

name: Cameron, Stewart
motto: "A mighty hunter is he and his prey is woman."
description: In 1901 Stewart came from Tyner, N. D., to the 'U' in order to delve into the mysteries of higher education. It is said that his high sense of honesty will not cause his conscience to rise up and smite him in the practice of law. Probably in the near future his vocation will be banking, but at present his avocation is passing pleasant moments in a cozy corner.

name: Frede, H. F.
motto: "In war was never Lion raged more fierce, In peace was gentle lamb more mild."
description: He comes and brings to the class the natural free and easy manner of that section, and to him the world is nothing but a material supply out of which he is to build his legal greatness. 'Sure' will sure become a great criminal lawyer, for the way he cross-examines the instructors, bespeaks genius.

name: Clifford, Thomas F.
motto: "What a fine man hath your 'Tailor' made you."
description: He received his preliminary education in Iowa, at the State Normal. He sees a fortune in the legal profession, and hopes to be a power in the judicial world. He is imbued with a spirit of eloquence, and is always ready to 'spout.'

name: Hyland, Thostein
motto: "He who is most slow in giving a promise is the most faithful in the performance of it."
hometown: Norway, present home at Washburn, N. D.
description: He has attended a number of Academies in Minnesota and South Dakota, and will culminate his educational career in North Dakota. Law is his one great ambition.
text:
"He who is most slow in giving a promise is the most faithful in the performance of it."

name: Farley, Ed. J.
motto: "Our married man."
description: He comes to us from Ada, Minn., having received his earlier education in Canada. Through his musical ability and charming ways, he has allured a fair maiden into the paths of matrimony. Farley is an authority on the association of facts. Judging from his eloquence, one would take him to be a descendant of Demosthenes.

name: Jamieson, James P.
motto: "If fame comes after death, I am in no hurry for it."
description: Since graduating in 1903, "Jimmy" has wandered in the wilderness, but this fall he crossed the Rubicon, entering the legal world. A good dentist was spoiled when he decided to become a lawyer, for he certainly has a good "pull" with the ladies. He is advocating a plan to have afternoon instead of morning sessions, as it is too strenuous to arise for class at nine o'clock. (Twiford is also in favor of this plan.)

name: Leonard, F. A.
motto: "No man can be a sound lawyer who is not well read in the laws of nature."
description: He was a teacher and principal of schools near Austin, Minn., his present home for eight years. His chief claim for notoriety is based upon his holding on the famous Chewing Gum case. His mustache pleases the ladies and protects them from the cold in winter.

name: Monson, L. P.
motto: "Eternity, thou pleasing dream of Thought."
clubs: Member of the Philomathian Debating Team
description: Our missionary's ambition is to go into the highways and byways and deliver the message of Justice. The introducer of Side Burns.
text:
"Eternity, thou pleasing dream of Thought."

name: Nash, R. F.
motto: "Behold, behold, everywhere be bold."
description: "Bob" is of the happy-go-lucky sort, but nevertheless a student of the law. Nash is a citizen of East Grand Forks, and received his preliminary education in that city. It is said that he is popular—with the young ladies.

name: O'Keefe, Henry
high school: Minto High School, St. Thomas College
motto: "Never do today what you can put off until tomorrow."
description: "Henry" was born at Minto some time ago, but came to Grand Forks this year with his parents. His ambition is to become director in Basketball at Vassar College, although it has been rumored that he sometimes attends the Law School. His specialty is common carriers and the rates charged on observation cars.

name: Pfeffer, John G.
motto: "Silence is golden."
clubs: Member of the Philomathian Debating Team
description: Pfeffer comes from Illinois, where he received his preliminary education at Peru. His present home is at Grand Forks. He has already astonished the class by the statement that, "A suit is always brought against the defendant."

name: Gunnlogson, Gunnlog B.
motto: "What I don't see, doesn't trouble me."
description: From Akra this prodigal first ventured into the world, and since that eventful day he has lived his life out there, until he decided that the legal profession needed another recruit, when he hied himself to our Law School, so that he might get the grounds of this, his life profession. A smart man, a wise man, and a manly man.

name: Twiford, G. W.
high school: Owatonna, Minn., High School. Canfield Business. Pillsbury Academy
motto: "She is my Shepard, I shall not want."
description: Guy is one of those industrious youths who never seem to tire of work, and many a night he burns the midnight oil in solving a legal (?) question; in fact he sometimes comes to class without having solved it. He was formerly secretary to Gov. Sarles, and has absorbed enough of the executive ability to be able to run a small sized state himself. A specialist on bills of fare and class schedules.

name: Johnson, E. S.
motto: "A toiler, a worker among men."
description: Johnson is a native of Fargo. Not only did he receive his preliminary education there, but spent a number of years at the Agricultural College. He is very enthusiastic and takes a great interest in class affairs. Although he is a hard worker, he generally has time to give one a cheerful smile.

name: Lee, Herman N.
motto: "A merry heart doeth good like a medicine."
clubs: prevailing spirit at the Law School dances
description: Herman is a graduate of Windom Institute at Montevideo, Minn., and was formerly a student at the "U." His present home is Grand Forks. His coming to us was a "godsend." Lee not only attends Law School, but also attends a private school in the city, where he is the only pupil and soon expects to graduate with honors.

name: Meagher, James R.
motto: "The reward for a thing well done, is to have done it."
description: Jim comes to us from Velva, N. D., where for four years he has been cashier of one of the banks there, but believing that he still had time to repent and lead an honest life he decided to become a lawyer. He is the Solon of our class, and is highly esteemed by all the members. A corporation lawyer is his ideal.

name: Manning, Joseph Michael
motto: "An honest man is the noblest work of God, but an Irish man is better than an honest man."
description: A great student of literature is Mike, and any sunny afternoon he may be found in the shade of a tree, deeply engaged in perusing that great legal work, "The Tracker Tracked, or, How Captain Sleuth Saved the Buttermilk." If we don't hear great things from Manning it will be because all the jobs on the police force have been taken before he gets there.

name: Tufton, Benjamin E.
motto: "Speech is great, but silence is greater."
description: "Tuffy" comes from Lyle, Minn. He is specializing on the game laws and will soon be authority on the question of hunting "deer" out of season, and the penalty attached. When not engaged in study he may be found at his official duties of custodian of the Bastille.

name: Nelson, Emil A.
motto: "Learn while we live, for when we die, -goodbye, little girl."
description: Though a native of Minnesota he was brought up and received his early education in Grand Forks county. He is a modest fellow, but he is ready and willing at all times to add a good word in behalf of the ideal woman.

name: Moum, Erick
high school: Concordia College, and the Normal Department at the University
motto: "My own thoughts are my best companions."
description: Moum's home is at Bottineau, N. D. Before entering the Law School he attended Concordia College, and the Normal Department at the University. He is a hard student, and will no doubt make a success, as a divorce lawyer.

"My own thoughts are my best companions."

name: Carter, Grace
motto: "Ever walking, ever talking, not with many but with one."
description: Miss Carter is a graduate of Grand Forks High School. A lover of athletics; she is an enthusiast in whatever she attempts. Her "underpasses" in basketball as well as her "overpasses" in literary lines have won her much recognition.

name: Baker, Louise
motto: "A college joke to cure the dumps."
clubs: Vice President of Y. W. C. A.
description: Louise, the "Toney-est" girl in her class, was born in Dixon, Ill., but at an early age moved to Emerado, N. D. Her present home is at Puyallup, Wash., where she will join her parents at the end of the school year. As the "best center in the state," her work in basketball needs no comment.

name: Dewolf, Nina
motto: "Dicko—Dickere—Dicksi—Dicktus—"
description: Graduate of the State Manual Training School. Her birthplace is Rushmore, Minn., but at an early age she migrated to Monango, N. D. She came here primarily to join our class, but incidentally to study Latin.

name: Johnson, Abbie
motto: "A voice like linked sweetness long drawn out."
description: Graduate of Grafton High School. Like all the Grafton girls Abbie is a general favorite, stands well in her classes and takes an active interest in Varsity life.
text:

name: Kristjansson, Kristbjorg
motto: "Industry is the key to success."
description: Miss Kristjansson was born at Mountain, N. D., which is her present home. She completed her High School work at the "U" and is known as a patient, energetic student, diligent in all things. She is one of the many Icelanders who have been examples of industry in our "U".

name: Murchie, Susie E.
motto: "Like virtue firm, like knowledge fair."
description: Inverness, Province of Quebec, was her birthplace; Hannah, N. D., is her present home; and Grand Forks her predicted future. Steady in the pursuit of duty, sympathetic in sorrow, in her righteous path there are no prints of evil to mar the beauty of its perfect state.

name: Omlie, Lillian
motto: "With a smile that is childlike and bland."
description: Grafton High School. Good natured, jolly and a friend to all who know her.
text:
"With a smile that is childlike and bland."

name: Covert, Emily C.
motto: "Her ways are the ways of pleasantness."
description: Graduate of State Manual Training School. Emily's aim in life is to become a politician. It has been rumored that she was quite a "hustler" during the winter campaign.
text:

name: Peterson, Mabel
motto: "She sleeps, my lady sleeps."
description: Miss Peterson, commonly known as "Pete" by her many friends, came from Edinburg to take up her preparatory and Normal work at the "U." Ready of hand and speech she is a worker as well as a talker.
text:

name: Cascaden, Myrtle
motto: "I can justly say with the hooked nose fellow of Rome, 'I came, I saw, I overcame.'"
description: Miss Cascaden was born in Manitoba. Her preparatory work was accomplished at the University, where she has won considerable fame in debate.
text:
"I can justly say with the hooked nose fellow of Rome, 'I came, I saw, I overcame.'"

name: Lytle, Blanche
motto: "In listening mood she seemed to stand, The guarding naiad of the strand."
description: From the Texan 90 degrees above, to Dakota's 40 degrees below, Miss Lytle came in the fall of 1906. She was born at Winchester, Kansas, and her high school work was taken at Topeka, Kan., and in LaPorte, Tex. She thoroughly believes in "Locke's hardening process," and evinced her courageous spirit by marked regularity in attendance during the blizzards of '07.

name: Hoadley, Effie
motto: "A purpose ever more before her eyes."
description: Her present home is at Josephine. She took up her preparatory work at the "U" and joined our class in '06. She has no affinity whatever for flunks.
text:
"A purpose ever more before her eyes."

name: Olstad, Olive
high school: Bruflat Academy, Portland, N. D., and from the Lutheran Ladies' Seminary, Red Wing, Minn.
motto: "So well to know her own, that what she wills to do or say, seems wisest, virtuous, discreetest, best."
description: A girl of many resources, Miss Olstad is a capable student and an agreeable companion.
text:
"So well to know her own, that what she wills to do or say, seems wisest, virtuous, discreetest, best."

name: Quam, Ida
motto: "She scarce can tell if she has loved or not, she of her heart no register has kept."
description: Ida is an old timer, having taken most of her "Prep" work at the "U." Although not the largest in the class she is one of the jolliest and best. A favorite in social circles of the "U," still her interest seems to be centered down town.

name: Turner, Echo
motto: "If there is delight in love, 'tis when I see that heart which others bleed for, bleeds for me."
description: One of the best known and jolliest of the "town" girls who took up work at the "U" in the fall of '06.
text:

name: Webb, Lulu
motto: "As merry as the day is long."
description: A cheerful disposition and a wide awake interest are her characteristics.
text:
"As merry as the day is long."

name: Wolff, Elsie
motto: "She knows her man, and when you rant and swear, can draw you to her with a single hair."
description: She is a dainty personage, whose sunny smile grows sweeter in view of rural scenes. A good student and one who takes an active interest in both literary and social circles.
text:

name: McLean, Jessie L.
motto: "A daughter of the gods, divinely tall and most divinely fair."
description: Though of Grafton she is not a grafter. Her amiable disposition wards off discord, while her friendly manner invites the doubting disputant to surrender.
text:

name: Daily, Gertrude
motto: "To win the secret of a weed's plain heart."
description: At present she camps out at the depot in order to catch the "first" train to the "U."

"To win the secret of a weed's plain heart."

name: Skinner, Gertrude
motto: "She that complies against her will, is of her own opinion still."
description: Her strength lies in her will. Her feminine weakness lends her moral strength. A determined spirit gives her power without detracting from her maiden charm of modesty.
